location.office_time_required: 3-4 days a week in one of our SF Bay Area offices

level_signals: 6+ years of experience as a product manager
Comp range $105K-$240K base, broad but top end consistent with senior IC
Expected to shape product roadmap and product team itself

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 6+ years of experience as a product manager at a B2B SaaS company.
You have a strong technical background and excel at analytical thinking
You are an excellent written and verbal communicator
You have a proven track record of taking ownership, taking initiative, and delivering results
You collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners
You have a learning and growth mindset
You are mission-first and understand that your success is measured by your product and team's success

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working on collaboration, productivity, or ML/ranking tools is a plus.

scope.surface: Knowledge management and work AI platform — search, assistant, and agentic capabilities
core_responsibilities: Spending a lot of time with our customers to deeply understand their knowledge needs
Developing key parts of our product roadmap, marrying customers' needs with our product vision
Empowering your team by giving context, setting direction, and building alignment
Driving customer-focused decisions, clear prioritization, and efficient execution
Coordinating cross-functionally with leadership, go-to-market teams, and other key stakeholders across the company
Building processes that will scale as our team and company go through rapid growth

compensation.equity_form: Certain roles may be eligible for variable compensation, equity, and benefits

green_flags: Clear product surface and mission articulated
Lean product team signals high ownership and impact potential
Company has strong external recognition (Fast Company, Forbes AI 50, CNBC Disruptor 50)
Comprehensive benefits package including 401k, home office stipend, education and wellness stipends
red_flags: Very wide compensation range ($105K-$240K) makes it difficult to gauge level or seniority being hired
Hybrid requirement of 3-4 days/week in SF Bay Area offices eliminates remote candidates
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international applicants
JD does not specify the particular product area or team this PM will own, making scope ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required + comp range)

scope.business_model (inferred from company description and B2B SaaS qualifier in requirements)
